prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG steampunk fantasy atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Iron Kingdoms / Full Metal Fantasy inspired steampunk world map, single huge primary continent.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye atlas view of Western Immoren-like industrial fantasy continent surrounded by stormy sea.
Subject: one enormous primary continent with complex realistic coastline: peninsulas, industrial gulfs, bays, capes, narrow straits, broken rocky shores, curved coasts, river deltas, inland seas, and varied landforms. Include mechanikal factory-cities, steam-vented ruins, warjack assembly ports, arcane storm fronts, rival nation borders, rail lines, river cities, blighted marshes, coal mountains, forests, tundra, deserts, wetlands, and coastal dockyards.
Style/medium: Iron Kingdoms continent map, steampunk fantasy aesthetic, mechanikal detail overlay, aged industrial parchment, highly detailed illustrated RPG sourcebook cartography.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, full single continent visible with generous ocean border, large high-resolution atlas feel, clean top-down readability.
Lighting/mood: full metal fantasy, steam and magic intertwined, warjack smoke plumes, arcane lightning, national warfare, mechaniks and alchemists at work in the margins as tiny map motifs.
Color palette: aged brass, soot grey, copper, storm blue, arcane cyan, muted green forests, iron red foundries, parchment tan.
Materials/textures: stained parchment, brass-etched coastlines, blueprint overlays, riveted border details, hand-painted terrain, fine cartographic ink.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Make the coastline natural, intricate, and realistic rather than a smooth blob.
